HomePage.title: CCUse
HomePage.slogan: One local endpoint that takes over multi-provider failover.
HomePage.description: Point Cursor, Claude Desktop, and OpenAI-compatible tools at a loopback API. CCUse chooses a healthy provider when each request arrives and switches to a backup route when the primary chain fails.

heroPreview.label: CCUse product preview
heroPreview.caption: A CCUse local proxy monitor window showing the local endpoint, request path, and provider status.
heroPreview.failoverText: When the primary provider slows down or fails, the request moves to the next available route.

HomePage.featuresTitle: Turn many AI providers into one reliable entry point.
HomePage.featuresDescription: From local auth and health checks to strategy selection, CCUse keeps resilience inside the proxy layer while clients keep using familiar OpenAI-compatible requests.

failover.title: Seamless failover
failover.description: When the primary provider times out, rate limits, or returns a 5xx, the proxy chooses the next available route within the request chain.

multiProvider.title: Multi-provider
multiProvider.description: Manage OpenAI, Claude, Gemini, relay services, and custom endpoints behind one local Base URL for every client.

healthCheck.title: Health checks
healthCheck.description: Background probes use model listings or low-cost requests, then cache Healthy, Degraded, and Down states for fast routing decisions.

smartStrategy.title: Smart strategy
smartStrategy.description: Route by priority, speed, cost, load balancing, or weighted scoring to match stability and budget goals.

monitoring.title: Live monitoring
monitoring.description: Track request volume, latency, success rate, switch history, and estimated cost without digging through raw logs.

crossPlatform.title: Cross-platform
crossPlatform.description: Built with Tauri 2 for macOS Apple Silicon, macOS Intel, and Windows x64 desktop installers.

architecture.title: Clients stay unchanged while resilience lives in the local proxy.
architecture.description: The homepage reuses the Mermaid architecture source from the product technical document: clients point at one local endpoint, while CCUse handles auth, parsing, provider selection, forwarding, and recovery inside the Rust proxy.
architecture.mobileAlt: CCUse architecture diagram: clients connect to the local proxy, which routes requests to multiple providers.

flowDescriptions.request: Clients keep sending OpenAI-compatible requests to the local Base URL.
flowDescriptions.selection: The proxy validates the local key, parses the request, and chooses a healthy provider by the active strategy.
flowDescriptions.recovery: Failed responses trigger automatic switching while the health checker keeps provider state fresh.

Metadata.description: Local API proxy for multi-provider AI routing, health checks, and automatic failover.

Footer.tagline: A local API proxy for resilient multi-provider AI clients.
